    <h1 {...{
      "id": "enterprise-distribution"
    }}>{`Enterprise Distribution`}</h1>
    <p>{`As an Enterprise or Team administrator, you can distribute UXP plugins to users across your organization through the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://adminconsole.adobe.com/"
      }}>{`Adobe Admin Console`}</a>{` or the `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`There are two primary ways to deploy UXP plugins at scale:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Via the `}<a parentName="strong" {...{
              "href": "https://adminconsole.adobe.com/"
            }}>{`Admin Console`}</a>{` with Creative Cloud Marketplace integration`}</strong><br parentName="p"></br>{`
`}<a parentName="p" {...{

`}<a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://helpx.adobe.com/enterprise/using/manage-extensions.html#others"
          }}>{`Manually place plugin files`}</a>{` in package folders and deploy them alongside Creative Cloud apps.`}</p>
      </li>
    </ol>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "1-deploy-plugins-through-the-admin-console"
    }}>{`1. Deploy plugins through the Admin Console`}</h2>
    <p>{`When building a package in the Admin Console (for `}<em parentName="p">{`Named User Licensing`}</em>{`):`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Select plugins from the Adobe Marketplace`}</strong>{` during package creation.`}<br parentName="li"></br>
        {`These plugins install automatically when the package is deployed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Control user permissions`}</strong>{`:`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Allow users to self-install plugins from the Marketplace via the Creative Cloud desktop app.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Restrict installation rights so only IT manages plugin deployment.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Automatic updates`}</strong>{`:`}<br parentName="li"></br>
        {`Marketplace plugins update through the Creative Cloud desktop app unless restricted.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This option works well when plugins are available on the Marketplace and you want streamlined, policy-driven distribution. See `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://helpx.adobe.com/enterprise/using/manage-extensions.html#named-user-licenses"
      }}>{`this Admin Console documentation page`}</a>{` for more details.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "2-bundle-ccx-plugins-in-managed-packages"
    }}>{`2. Bundle `}<inlineCode parentName="h2">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` plugins in managed packages`}</h2>
    <p>{`If your plugin isn't listed in the Marketplace—for example, for internal distribution:`}</p>
    <ul>

      <li parentName="ul">{`A folder will be generated in the package structure; place your plugin’s `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` files in this folder.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`When the package is deployed, the included plugins install automatically with the Creative Cloud apps.`}</li>

    <p>{`See `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "important-considerations"
    }}>{`Important Considerations`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`App dependency`}</strong>{`: Plugins only install if the corresponding host app (e.g., Premiere) is also present in the package or already installed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Error handling`}</strong>{`: A plugin installation failure does `}<em parentName="li">{`not`}</em>{` block the package installation.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Activation`}</strong>{`: If a plugin requires the host app to restart, users must restart the app before the plugin becomes active.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Unique IDs`}</strong>{`: If you plan to distribute a plugin `}<em parentName="li">{`both`}</em>{` internally and through the Creative Cloud Marketplace, create a clone of your plugin with a different ID as explained `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../package/index.md#mind-your-plugins-id"

      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UPIA tool`}</strong>{`: An administrator can `}<a parentName="li" {...{

        }}>{`this FAQ`}</a>{`, in environments with limited internet access or under Adobe's Feature Restricted Licensing, it is still possible to install and use UXP plugins using `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` files and the UPIA tool.`}</li>
    </ul>
